During the 24, uh, 23 24 25, uh, financial year, which this annual review is underway, the government is incredibly grateful for their commitment to delivering care for patients and putting patients first.

The annual report demonstrates that while progress has been made, there remains significant challenges in the health system. The most pressing of these is wait times, which remain the number one clinical risk. And one of this government's top priorities to address too many New Zealanders are still waiting too long the care that they need. This must change. That's why this government is focused on restoring the basics, rebuilding a health system that is transparent, accountable, and relentlessly focused on delivering outcomes for patients.

We've reinstated national health targets to drive performance. We've launched an elective surgery boost, which saw more procedures delivered in the last quarter of the 24 25 financial year than in any quarter. In the past decade, we've begun work to expand, extend urgent care services, especially in rural and regional communities.

We're also making changes to restore local decision making. The previous government's centralized bureaucratic health reforms, stripped autonomy from communities. We returning decision making to health New Zealand's regional and district levels where people closest to patients are empowered to act. A health system that is nationally and regionally planned must be delivered locally by the people who understand the community the best.

We also, at the same time, expecting value for money. The government is investing record funding in health and New Zealanders rightfully expect results. That means, uh, delivering more care for patients, improving access and outcomes, and focusing on frontline service delivery. And the past year, we've increased the frontline workforce by around 750 full-time equivalents.

We've also seen a sharp reduction in the use of, uh, consultants with spending down significantly meaning more money for frontline care. And short, while progress is underway, we know hard work is far finished. We'll keep pushing to deliver the outcomes. New Zealanders deserve shorter wait times, more accessible services, and a health system that puts patients first. Thank You. Thank you.
